title: Custom EQ slider positions persist after closing and reopening EQ settings screen
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the connected device name (e.g., Voyager Free 60+ or Surround 80) to open Device Settings.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom' EQ preset to select it.
Adjust the first slider (e.g., 100 Hz band) to a specific non-default value (e.g., +6 dB).
Adjust the second slider (e.g., 300 Hz band) to a different specific value (e.g., -4 dB).
Adjust the third slider (e.g., 1 kHz band) to a specific value (e.g., +3 dB).
Adjust the fourth slider (e.g., 3 kHz band) to a specific value (e.g., -2 dB).
Adjust the fifth slider (e.g., 8 kHz band) to a specific value (e.g., +5 dB).
Tap the 'Save EQ' button.
Navigate back to the Device Settings screen (tap back arrow or 'Audio Settings' header).
Reopen Equalizer by navigating Audio Settings → Equalizer again.
Observe the positions of all five EQ sliders.
expected: All five EQ slider positions should match the values that were saved in step 10. The 100 Hz band shows +6 dB, 300 Hz band shows -4 dB, 1 kHz band shows +3 dB, 3 kHz band shows -2 dB, and 8 kHz band shows +5 dB. The 'Custom' preset remains selected.

title: Custom EQ slider positions persist after app force-kill and relaunch
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the connected device name to open Device Settings.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom' EQ preset.
Set slider values to: 100 Hz = +8 dB, 300 Hz = -6 dB, 1 kHz = +2 dB, 3 kHz = 0 dB, 8 kHz = +7 dB.
Tap 'Save EQ' button.
Force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe away from recent apps on Android).
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
Wait for the app to connect to the device automatically.
Tap the connected device name to open Device Settings.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Observe the positions of all five EQ sliders.
expected: All five EQ slider positions should match the saved values: 100 Hz = +8 dB, 300 Hz = -6 dB, 1 kHz = +2 dB, 3 kHz = 0 dB, 8 kHz = +7 dB. The 'Custom' preset is still selected.

title: Custom EQ slider positions persist after Bluetooth disconnect and reconnect
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the connected device name to open Device Settings.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom' EQ preset.
Set slider values to: 100 Hz = +4 dB, 300 Hz = +1 dB, 1 kHz = -3 dB, 3 kHz = +6 dB, 8 kHz = -5 dB.
Tap 'Save EQ' button.
Navigate back to the main Device Settings screen.
Disconnect the Bluetooth device (either from phone Bluetooth settings or by powering off the device).
Wait 5 seconds.
Reconnect the Bluetooth device (power on and wait for auto-reconnect, or manually pair).
Open Poly Studio Mobile app and tap the connected device name.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Observe the positions of all five EQ sliders.
expected: All five EQ slider positions should match the saved values: 100 Hz = +4 dB, 300 Hz = +1 dB, 1 kHz = -3 dB, 3 kHz = +6 dB, 8 kHz = -5 dB. The 'Custom' preset is still selected.

title: Custom EQ slider positions persist after switching to another preset and back to Custom
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the connected device name to open Device Settings.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom' EQ preset.
Set slider values to: 100 Hz = +10 dB, 300 Hz = -8 dB, 1 kHz = +5 dB, 3 kHz = -3 dB, 8 kHz = +2 dB.
Tap 'Save EQ' button.
Tap a different preset (e.g., 'Bass boost' or 'Voice clarity').
Verify the EQ sliders change to the selected preset's values.
Tap the 'Custom' preset again.
Observe the positions of all five EQ sliders.
expected: When switching back to the 'Custom' preset, all five EQ slider positions should return to the previously saved values: 100 Hz = +10 dB, 300 Hz = -8 dB, 1 kHz = +5 dB, 3 kHz = -3 dB, 8 kHz = +2 dB. The custom slider values are not lost or reset when switching to another preset and back.

title: Custom EQ slider positions persist after device power cycle
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the connected device name to open Device Settings.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom' EQ preset.
Set slider values to: 100 Hz = +7 dB, 300 Hz = -5 dB, 1 kHz = 0 dB, 3 kHz = +4 dB, 8 kHz = -2 dB.
Tap 'Save EQ' button.
Navigate back to the main Device Settings screen.
Power off the Bluetooth device (hold power button until device turns off).
Wait 10 seconds.
Power on the Bluetooth device.
Wait for the device to reconnect to the phone automatically.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app and tap the connected device name.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Observe the positions of all five EQ sliders.
expected: All five EQ slider positions should match the saved values: 100 Hz = +7 dB, 300 Hz = -5 dB, 1 kHz = 0 dB, 3 kHz = +4 dB, 8 kHz = -2 dB. The 'Custom' preset is still selected. The EQ settings survive a full device power cycle.

title: Custom EQ slider extreme values persist after saving and reopening EQ screen
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the connected device name to open Device Settings.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom' EQ preset.
Set all five EQ sliders to their maximum value (e.g., +12 dB or +10 dB depending on device).
Tap 'Save EQ' button.
Navigate back to Device Settings.
Reopen Equalizer by navigating Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Observe the positions of all five EQ sliders.
Navigate back and reopen again.
Set all five EQ sliders to their minimum value (e.g., -12 dB or -10 dB).
Tap 'Save EQ' button.
Navigate back to Device Settings.
Reopen Equalizer by navigating Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Observe the positions of all five EQ sliders.
expected: First reopen: All five EQ sliders should still be at their maximum values. Second reopen: All five EQ sliders should still be at their minimum values. Extreme boundary values are correctly persisted.

title: Custom EQ slider positions persist after phone rotation during EQ screen
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the connected device name to open Device Settings.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom' EQ preset.
Set slider values to: 100 Hz = +5 dB, 300 Hz = -3 dB, 1 kHz = +4 dB, 3 kHz = -1 dB, 8 kHz = +6 dB.
Tap 'Save EQ' button.
Rotate the phone from portrait to landscape orientation.
Observe the positions of all five EQ sliders.
Rotate the phone back to portrait orientation.
Observe the positions of all five EQ sliders.
Navigate back to Device Settings.
Reopen Equalizer by navigating Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Observe the positions of all five EQ sliders.
expected: After rotation to landscape: All five EQ slider positions should still show the saved values. After rotation back to portrait: All five EQ slider positions should still show the saved values. After closing and reopening: All five EQ slider positions should still match the saved values (100 Hz = +5 dB, 300 Hz = -3 dB, 1 kHz = +4 dB, 3 kHz = -1 dB, 8 kHz = +6 dB). Configuration changes do not cause slider reset.

title: Custom EQ slider positions persist after app backgrounding and foregrounding
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the connected device name to open Device Settings.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom' EQ preset.
Set slider values to: 100 Hz = +9 dB, 300 Hz = -7 dB, 1 kHz = +3 dB, 3 kHz = -4 dB, 8 kHz = +1 dB.
Tap 'Save EQ' button.
Press the Home button to background the app.
Wait 30 seconds.
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app from the app launcher or recent apps.
Tap the connected device name to open Device Settings.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Observe the positions of all five EQ sliders.
expected: All five EQ slider positions should match the saved values: 100 Hz = +9 dB, 300 Hz = -7 dB, 1 kHz = +3 dB, 3 kHz = -4 dB, 8 kHz = +1 dB. The 'Custom' preset is still selected. Backgrounding and foregrounding the app does not cause slider reset.

title: Custom EQ slider positions persist after saving without closing EQ screen (immediate verification)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the connected device name to open Device Settings.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom' EQ preset.
Set slider values to: 100 Hz = +2 dB, 300 Hz = +5 dB, 1 kHz = -6 dB, 3 kHz = +8 dB, 8 kHz = -3 dB.
Tap 'Save EQ' button.
Without navigating away, immediately observe the positions of all five EQ sliders.
Wait 10 seconds.
Observe the positions of all five EQ sliders again.
expected: Immediately after tapping 'Save EQ', all five EQ slider positions should remain at the saved values (100 Hz = +2 dB, 300 Hz = +5 dB, 1 kHz = -6 dB, 3 kHz = +8 dB, 8 kHz = -3 dB). The sliders should not snap back to default positions after saving. After 10 seconds, the values should still be unchanged.

title: Custom EQ slider positions persist after rapid successive saves
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the connected device name to open Device Settings.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap the 'Custom' EQ preset.
Set slider values to: 100 Hz = +3 dB, 300 Hz = -1 dB, 1 kHz = +7 dB, 3 kHz = -5 dB, 8 kHz = +4 dB.
Tap 'Save EQ' button.
Immediately change slider values to: 100 Hz = -2 dB, 300 Hz = +6 dB, 1 kHz = -4 dB, 3 kHz = +9 dB, 8 kHz = -7 dB.
Tap 'Save EQ' button again.
Immediately change slider values to: 100 Hz = +1 dB, 300 Hz = 0 dB, 1 kHz = -2 dB, 3 kHz = +3 dB, 8 kHz = -1 dB.
Tap 'Save EQ' button again.
Navigate back to Device Settings.
Reopen Equalizer by navigating Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Observe the positions of all five EQ sliders.
expected: All five EQ slider positions should match the last saved values: 100 Hz = +1 dB, 300 Hz = 0 dB, 1 kHz = -2 dB, 3 kHz = +3 dB, 8 kHz = -1 dB. Rapid successive saves correctly persist the most recently saved state without corruption or reset to default.
